'''Abatrang''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] occupying the seventh [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Atrig]] in the [[Atrig system]],<ref name="Collection" /> which was part of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was an uninhabitable [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] with eighteen [[Moon/Legends|moons]] and had an [[Ammonia/Legends|ammonia]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] near Abatrang was among the sites where [["Carrion eaters"|"carrion eaters,"]] a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] who posed a threat to individuals operating as [[scavenger]]s, were active.<ref name="Collection" />

==Behind the scenes==
Abatrang was first mentioned in the [[1992]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] sourcebook ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]'', which was written by [[John Terra]] and released by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. One roleplaying adventure idea presented to players suggests that,<ref name="PG2">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]''</ref> in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]'' is set two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which corresponds to [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corellian corvette]] ''[[Empire's Twilight]]'' becomes stranded after being attacked by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in the planet's vicinity and pulled in by the world's [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]. After the forty-five crewmembers land on the world in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape module]], the player characters must work to save the crew by venturing onto Abatrang's surface—which contradicts the same book also describing the planet as a gas giant. This article assumes the latter description is correct.<ref name="PG2" />

''Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two'' also spells the planet's name as "Abatrarg" once, though this article assumes that the more commonly used spelling of "Abatrang" is correct.<ref name="PG2" /> The lone misspelling is additionally present in [[Bill Smith]]'s [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]'', which included a partial reprint of the content of ''Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two''.<ref name="Collection" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Atrig [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Abatrang, in grid square J-14.<ref name="TEA" />

*<!-- 1992-06-XX -->''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Two]]'' {{1stm}}
